findings.description: ConfigLoader and its private methods (_read_file, _read_env, _coerce) lack docstrings.
TOCTOU race between os.path.exists and open, and exists() also passes for directories, so open can still raise FileNotFoundError or IsADirectoryError despite the guard.

yaml.load is called without an explicit Loader instead of yaml.safe_load, enabling arbitrary Python object deserialization (potential RCE) if the config file is attacker-influenced on PyYAML<5.1 or FullLoader versions vulnerable to CVE-2020-14343.
On PyYAML>=6.0 the Loader argument is mandatory, so yaml.load(f) raises TypeError and every load() call crashes depending on the installed dependency version.
Malformed YAML raises an uncaught yaml.YAMLError, inconsistent with the graceful empty-dict degradation used for missing files and non-mapping roots.
An empty YAML file parses to None and is logged at ERROR as a non-mapping root, misclassifying a legitimate empty config file as an error.
YAML mapping keys are returned unvalidated, so non-string keys (e.g., bare on:/yes: parsed as boolean True) violate the dict[str, Any] contract and can never align with lowercased env overlay keys.
Every APP_-prefixed environment variable is merged into the config without a known-key whitelist, allowing unrelated or attacker-set env vars to inject arbitrary config keys.

_coerce force-converts any numeric- or boolean-looking env string (e.g., a password like 123456 or version 1.10) to int/float/bool with no opt-out, silently changing value types downstream code expects to be strings.
dict(DEFAULTS) is a shallow copy, so the returned config shares the mutable feature_flags dict with module-global DEFAULTS and caller mutations pollute all subsequent loads.
Merge order contradicts the documented precedence: the file overlay is applied after the env overlay, so config-file values silently override environment variables instead of the reverse.
An INFO-level log line is emitted on every load(), which combined with per-access reloading produces log spam on hot paths such as feature_enabled checks.
Optional[Any] is redundant since Any already includes None; plain Any suffices.
get()/require()/feature_enabled() call load() each time, re-reading and re-parsing the YAML file and rescanning the entire environment on every single key access with no caching.
require() cannot distinguish a missing key from one explicitly set to null/None in the file, raising a spurious KeyError for legitimately-None values.
feature_flags is not type-checked before use, so a scalar value (e.g., from APP_FEATURE_FLAGS env coercion or malformed YAML) makes flags.get raise AttributeError.
